content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the branch diff against `origin/master`, read the controller, CLI, fs-store, engine, and success-reconciliation changes end-to-end, and found two correctness issues: a crash window in success reconciliation that wrongly escalates to `needs_operator`, and a regression that makes final completed-bead sync non-idempotent.

## Amendments

1. Bug: the success-reconciliation flow closes and syncs the bead before the controller can durably settle into a resumable post-run state ([src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:142-167]). On resume, any `claimed`, `running`, or `reconciling` controller whose bead is already closed is treated as stale divergence and forced into `needs_operator` ([src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:806-812], [src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:844-847], [src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:876-879]). A crash anywhere in that normal close/sync/reconcile window therefore strands an otherwise idempotent success path behind manual intervention. Either move the controller into a dedicated resumable reconciliation state before `br close`/`br sync`, or relax resume validation so this post-close window can continue automatically.
   Rationale: This breaks the promised stop/resume behavior for a realistic crash point in the success path and can require operator recovery even though the remaining reconciliation work is already designed to be replay-safe.
2. Bug: duplicate completion syncs on the final bead are no longer idempotent. Both the CLI sync path and the daemon reconciliation path unconditionally try to re-enter `reconciling` for a completed run ([src/cli/run.rs:585-595], [src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:289-297]), but `sync_controller_task_reconciling` hard-errors once the controller has already reached `completed` ([src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:530-534]). Because successful final-bead reconciliation itself moves the controller to `completed` ([src/cli/run.rs:653-674], [src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:381-397]), rerunning `ralph-burning run sync-milestone` or replaying the same completed reconciliation becomes a hard failure instead of a no-op/repair.
   Rationale: The exposed repair/replay paths should tolerate repeated completion syncs. As written, a second sync attempt against an already reconciled final bead fails even though the lineage and milestone update helpers are intentionally idempotent.
